translated: Then Jacob was afraid and distressed; so he divided the people who were with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two groups.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Yakob hunuu wɔn no, ɔkaa sɛ: Nyankopɔn asafo nie. Na ɔtoo hɔ din: Maha-naim.
translated: When Jacob saw them, he said: This is God's camp. And he named the place Mahanaim.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Mesrɛ wo, yi me firi me nua Esau nsam, ɛfiri sɛ mesuro no sɛ ɔbɛba abɛku me, ne ɛnanom ne wɔn mma nyinaa.
translated: I beg you, rescue me from my brother Esau, because I am afraid that he will come and kill me, along with my wives and all my children.

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Na ɔse: Mɛma wɔde abirekyie ba afiri mmoa mu abrɛ wo. Na ɔse: Ɛnneɛ ma me awowadeɛ kɔsi sɛ wode bɛba.
translated: And he said: I will have someone bring a goat from among the animals to you. And he said: Now let my wife go until you bring it.

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: ewiem nnomaa mu nso fa nson nson, anini ne abedeɛ, na wɔn ase nnya nkwa asase nyinaa ani.
translated: Among the birds of the air, divide them into sevens, the clean and the unclean, and let their blood be poured upon all the living earth.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Na Adam ne ne yere daeɛ bio, na ɔwoo ɔbabarima, na ɔtoo ne din Set sɛ: Onyankopɔn de ɔba foforɔ asi me Habel a Kain kumm no no ananmu.
translated: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, saying: God has given me another child in place of Abel, whom Cain killed.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Na Laban sɔree anɔpatutu, na ɔfee ne nananom ne ne mmammaa no ano hyiraa wɔn, na ɔsii mu sane kɔɔ ne kurom.
translated: Then Laban got up early in the morning and kissed his grandchildren and daughters goodbye, and set out and returned to his home.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Yakob sii mu kɔɔ Sukot, na ɔsii dan maa ne ho, na ɔbobɔɔ asese maa ne mmoa. Yei nti na wɔfrɛ hɔ Sukot.
translated: Then Jacob got up and went to Sukkot, and he built a house for himself, and he made shelters for his livestock. That is why the place is called Sukkot.

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: Owura, tie yɛn! Okunini bi ne wo wɔ yɛn mu. Yɛn ada mu deɛ wopɛ no, sie w’amu no wɔ mu. Yɛn mu biara renkame wo ne da, sɛ nsie w’amu wɔ mu.
translated: Master, listen to us! There is a man among us. In our room, the one you desire, hide him in your heart. None of us will betray you and the room, if you keep him hidden in your heart.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.

translated: And God said: Let there be lights in the sky to separate day from night, and let them serve as signs to mark seasons, days, and years.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Na Yakob dɔ Rahel, na ɔkaa sɛ: Wo ba kumaa Rahel nti mɛsom wo mfeɛ nson.
translated: And Jacob loved Rachel, and he said: Because of your younger daughter Rachel, I will serve you for seven years.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Na ɔsomfoɔ no faa ne wura yoma mu du, na ɔkɔe, na ɔkura ne wura nnepa ahodoɔ nyinaa bi. Na ɔsɔre sii mu kɔɔ Mesopotamia, Nahor kuro mu.
translated: Then the servant took ten of his master’s camels and went, carrying some of all his master’s good things. He set out and went to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nahor.

ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”

translated: No one is greater in this house than I am, and he has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. So why should I commit this great evil and sin against God?

translated: Then Joseph’s master took him and put him in prison, where the king’s prisoners were kept, and he stayed there. God was with Joseph.

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.

translated: Because of Rachel, Jacob served for seven years, and because of the love he had for her, it seemed to him like a few days.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.

translated: And you will live with us, and this land that lies before you, live on it and work it to get its produce.

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Na ɔbaa no hunuu sɛ dua no yɛ sɛ wɔdie, na ɛyɛ aniwa fɛ, ɛyɛ akɔnnɔ, na ɛbɛte ani, enti ɔtee n’aba bi, na ɔdii, na ɔde bi maa ne kunu a ɔka ne ho no nso, na ɔdii.
translated: Then the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, so she took some of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate.
